rulings.Case Title: MILAN BACOKA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 8, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SAM HASTY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 8, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4, Defendant Brand Energy Services of California, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and denied as to issue 3 (premises owner/contractor liability). Defendant did not satisfy its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Hasty was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant making it liable under the premises owner/contractor liability theory. Defendant¿s failure to demonstrate that plaintiffs responded to written contention discovery precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production to negate an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claim. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Mr. Singletary¿s declaration are sustained. The court did not consider the evidence submitted with defendant¿s reply. (See San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308.) Plaintiffs have dismissed their negligence and strict liability causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HERSHEL HENRY STRONG VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 8, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 8, Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Oberta¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HERSHEL HENRY STRONG VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 8, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 8, Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of plaintiff, Mr. Johnson and Mr. Walters are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Oberta¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON DUNN et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 8, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 7, Defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc.¿s demurrer to the first, second, third, fourth, sixth and seventh causes of action in plaintiffs¿ amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiffs¿ causes of action contain allegations sufficient to state the bases for their claims against defendant. This includes sufficient product identification under Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, and sufficient allegations as to the false representation (Restatement 402-B) and intentional tort (Civil Code §§ 1708, 1709 and 1710) causes of action, as well as loss of consortium and survival. This court previously overruled defendant¿s special demurrer to the loss of consortium cause of action. Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
